mechanism_narrative: Persistent increases in nonfarm payroll employment (PAYEMS) signal improving labor market conditions that boost consumer spending and corporate earnings, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC of PAYEMS monthly change against forward 20-day returns is negative or less than 0.05 over any rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate (UNRATE) falls below 8.0%, it indicates that the economy has passed a critical threshold of labor market slack reduction, and further declines signal sustainable growth that supports positive equity returns over the subsequent 60 trading days.
failure_condition: After UNRATE crosses below 8.0%, the average 60-day forward return is negative or less than the unconditional average return.

mechanism_narrative: The joint behavior of very low short-term interest rates (FEDFUNDS) and rising industrial production (INDPRO) signals an accommodative monetary stance combined with real economic expansion, which compresses discount rates and increases earnings expectations, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
regime_hint: low interest rate and expanding output regime
failure_condition: In periods when FEDFUNDS is below 0.5% and INDPRO is above 100, the forward 20-day return is not significantly positive (one-sided t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: In a low inflation regime (CPIAUCSL below 220), increases in industrial production (INDPRO) reflect genuine real growth without overheating, which raises corporate profits and pushes equity prices higher over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When CPIAUCSL is below 220, the rank correlation between INDPRO monthly change and forward 20-day returns is less than 0.05 over any 2-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts (HOUST) indicate strengthening residential investment and consumer confidence, which forecasts increased economic activity and supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as a leading indicator.
failure_condition: The correlation between HOUST monthly change and forward 60-day returns is negative or below 0.10 over any rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) surging above 370,000 signals a sharp acceleration in consumer spending that often surprises markets upward, leading to positive short-term S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as positive sentiment and earnings revisions occur.
failure_condition: In the 5 trading days following an RSAFS print above 370,000, the average return is not positive or the win rate is below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: The combined improvement in labor market (falling UNRATE) and industrial production (rising INDPRO) provides a robust confirmation of broad-based economic expansion, which drives corporate earnings growth and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: When both UNRATE is falling and INDPRO is rising month-over-month, the forward 60-day return is negative or zero more than 40% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: In the zero lower bound regime (FEDFUNDS ≤ 0.5%), rising inflation as measured by CPIAUCSL is interpreted negatively because it may force premature monetary tightening, which reduces equity valuations and leads to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In periods when FEDFUNDS ≤ 0.5%, the rank correlation between CPIAUCSL monthly change and forward 20-day returns is non-negative over any 2-year rolling window.
